CHARACTER CREATION
Path; Clan character $ Social Standing Attribute must equal his Minimum: After meeting  all the minimums_ you can use your remain- ing Character Points t0 raise Attributes further
Clan Phenotypes: In Mw3, character' = phenotype is his or her genetic design crafted by Clan scientists The Clans use three   phenotypes; each represented trait: Elementals MechWarriors and Fighter Pilots. Phenotypes include only those characteristics genetically built in_ Training and indoctrination are covered by the character'$ affiliation and Life Path Unlike other traits phenotypes do not cost Character Points. char- acter IS trueborn Clan warrior, he will receive Clan pheno type in Stage 1.
Purchasing Tralts After the Life Path, you may want t0 give your character more traits t0 round out him or her. All positive traits list point value which must be paid out of your Character Points Negative traits list negative values; when you buy negative trait for your character, you get more points to spend on posi- tive traits or higher Attributes_ For example_ the Amnesia Trait value of - If you take it for your character; YOu can also take 3-point positive trait such as Toughness or three 1-point traits , Or YOu may spend three extra points on Attributes_ Iist of traits iS included for reference below: Complete descriptions of all the traits appear in the Traits section_ Traits marked on the list in boldface can only be acquired dur- ing initial character creation character can sometimes end up with equivalent  effects  through game play; such as earning money (Wealth) r making friends (Contact), but these are not represented by traits paid for with Character Points_ Other traits can be acquired after character creation Character Points can also be spent t0 get rid of negative traits that were picked up during the Life Path However, there should be justification in the character' $ story for how this occurred; if the gamemaster feels the explanation isn: ade quate , the trait may not be removed: Negative   traits can be acquired after character creation through game play; but they do not earn Character Points For example . your character suffers grievous injury that costs him his hand he does not get to purchase three points of pOs: itivve traits_ Positive traits acquired through game play must be paid for with Experience Points (see 2101. order t0 prevent players from loading down their charac ters with dozens of minor  negative traits order to create super-characters the character creation system offers dimin shing returns for buying lots of negative traits Only the two neg ative traits with the highest values that you purchase for your character are worth their full value; all others you buy are only worth half. Keep track of half points; any odd half point left over at the end of this step lost.
